codeReview.summary: The n8n-mcp plugin fix is spec-compliant. The register() function now correctly calls ctx.register_tool() for all 4 MCP tools via a loop over get_tool_definitions(). The dead wait_for_result parameter has been removed from mcp_bridge.py. The registration pattern matches the google_meet and spotify plugin conventions.

toolRegistrationLoop.evidence: __init__.py lines 147-155: register() iterates plugin.mcp_bridge.get_tool_definitions() and calls ctx.register_tool(name, toolset, schema, handler, check_fn, emoji) for each tool definition. All 4 tools are registered: n8n_health_check, n8n_list_workflows, n8n_execute_workflow, n8n_get_execution.

waitForResultRemoved.evidence: grep for 'wait_for_result' across sidecars/hermes-agent-nous/plugins/general/n8n-mcp/ returned zero matches. The dead parameter has been fully removed.

patternConformance.evidence: Registration pattern matches google_meet (plugins/google_meet/__init__.py:82-90) and spotify (plugins/spotify/__init__.py:58-66): both iterate a tool list and call ctx.register_tool(name, toolset, schema, handler, check_fn, emoji). The n8n-mcp plugin uses the same kwargs signature with toolset='n8n-mcp'.

testResults.evidence: 37/37 tests passed via python3 -m unittest tests.plugins.test_n8n_mcp_plugin -v. Key test test_register_entry_point verifies exactly 4 tools are registered with correct names and toolset.

summary: ✅ Spec compliant. All 4 MCP tools are registered via ctx.register_tool() in the register() entry point. The dead wait_for_result parameter is gone from mcp_bridge.py. Registration follows the established google_meet/spotify plugin pattern. All 37 unit tests pass.
